description: In Victoria, learner and probationary (P1/P2) drivers must have a BAC of 0.00. Fully licensed drivers generally have a BAC limit of 0.05.
Children must be restrained in an approved, appropriate child restraint for their age/size; an adult seatbelt alone may not provide correct protection.
A clearway means you must not stop or park on that side of the road during the clearway times shown on signs.
Defensive driving means anticipating others’ mistakes, scanning ahead, and keeping a safe buffer space so you can respond early to hazards.
A double continuous centre line means you must not cross or straddle the lines.
If an emergency vehicle is using lights/siren, move out of its path and let it pass safely; don’t follow closely behind it to get through traffic.
Fatigue can affect driving like alcohol: slower reactions and poor decisions. If you feel drowsy, stop and rest before continuing.
Under good conditions, keep at least a two-second gap behind the vehicle in front; increase the gap in poor conditions.
In wet conditions, increase your following distance (e.g., to about four seconds) because stopping distances increase and grip is reduced.
At intersections with no signs/signals, give way according to the rules (commonly: give way to the vehicle on your right).
A hook turn is a special right turn used in some Melbourne CBD intersections to avoid blocking trams; you typically turn right from the left lane when signs indicate.
Before changing lanes, signal, check mirrors and blind spots, and only move when it’s safe—lane markings must be obeyed.
Learners must drive only with an appropriately licensed supervising driver seated beside them; if supervision is not effective, stop safely.
Learner drivers must display L plates clearly so they are visible on the vehicle while driving.
Learner drivers in Victoria must not use a mobile phone in any way while driving.
No Stopping means you must not stop at all (except for traffic/emergency). No Parking may allow brief pick-up/drop-off if permitted.
On multi-lane roads, overtaking on the left is allowed when vehicles are in separate marked lanes and it is safe.
Painted traffic islands/chevrons indicate an area you must not drive on (unless markings/signs specifically allow).
Parking too close to corners/intersections can block visibility and is restricted; always follow signs/road rules to keep sight lines clear.
When turning, you must give way to pedestrians crossing the road you are entering (and stay alert near crossings and busy areas).
Stop at railway crossings when lights flash or boom gates are down; never enter if you can’t clear the tracks safely.
Give way to vehicles already in the roundabout; use indicators correctly (including signalling left when exiting where required).
In signed school zones, lower speed limits apply during the times shown; slow down even if children aren’t visible.
You must stop for trams when required (e.g., when doors are open) and give way to pedestrians around tram stops/safety zones.
U-turns are only allowed where permitted (not prohibited by signs/markings) and must be done safely while giving way to others.
